lines.text: (not looking up) [flatly] I'm gonna shut it off. There's nothing on anyway, it's the weather and then more weather after that.
I already watched the whole thing this morning, on my phone, before I even got here.
Your four guys. [pause] Somebody set it to music.
...and this one's been making the rounds all morning, a charming, if a little off-key, moment out of somewhere in Nebraska.
(auto-tuned) Come on now, grandpa, get you up on the beat.
(dry) [flatly] That boy's got a real nice voice on him.

...the Accord confirming this morning that the program will now take in the Apollo suits as well. [pause] One museum suit to travel with each flag. The first, they tell us, with its flag, to the people of Iran.
(off the anchor) [quietly] That's the A-seven-L. Twenty-one layers in that suit. [pause] Nobody ever built a better one.

(to the phone) Okay. Boomer diner check, day whatever, forty-one. Coffee here is a dollar ninety. One ninety, for a cup. And my landlord just put me up four hundred a month, four hundred, and this whole place is full of guys who bought their houses for like a dollar.
(to the phone) [sarcastic] Like nobody in here has ever once heard the word rent. I'm serious.
(muffled) Got a delivery here for a booth three?
(to the phone) ...and don't even get me started on the parking out here.
(to Earlene) They got rain coming in Thursday, they're saying. Maybe on into Friday.
